2026-07-14 09:12:38

Yesterday (Monday) - The market entered the new week on a relatively cautious note as investors weighed recent gains against upcoming economic data and the start of earnings season. Although the major indexes experienced a modest pullback, the broader market trend remained constructive, and TurboRegime's RiskOn signal remained firmly intact through the closing bell. As always, the strategy focuses on confirmed end-of-day market conditions rather than reacting to intraday volatility.

Today (Tuesday) - Pre-market futures are indicating a modestly firmer open as investors prepare for another active day of economic data and corporate earnings. While overnight trading provides an early indication of market sentiment, TurboRegime does not make allocation decisions based on futures or pre-market activity. Instead, the strategy will continue monitoring market conditions throughout today's session and will recommend a portfolio change only if the regime signal remains confirmed at today's close.

Current Risk Regime - RiskOn remains in effect, active since July 1. Following yesterday's close, the current trade stands at a gain of 0.3%, while the maximum drawdown since entering the position remains just -0.2%. Although the market surrendered a portion of last week's gains, the overall character of the trade remains constructive. Temporary pullbacks are a normal part of developing market trends, and the model continues to prioritize confirmation over prediction. By requiring end-of-day confirmation before making allocation changes, TurboRegime seeks to avoid reacting to short-lived market noise while remaining positioned for longer-term opportunities.

Hold or Trade - HOLD. No portfolio changes are indicated following yesterday's close. Current allocation remains 85% SPY, 15% SHV, and 0% SPXL. The strategy continues to participate in the broader market while maintaining a modest defensive allocation until market momentum either strengthens enough to support a more aggressive posture or weakens sufficiently to warrant a defensive transition.

Live Performance - Live tracking has now reached 104 days since beginning on April 1, 2026. Through yesterday's close, TurboRegime has executed just six live trades while producing a cumulative return of 32.6%, compared with 14.7% for a buy-and-hold investment in SPY over the same period. The strategy continues to outperform SPY by 17.9 percentage points. Perhaps equally encouraging, live execution continues to closely mirror historical expectations. Since April 1, the live portfolio has returned 31.8%, compared with 32.4% for the historical backtest over the same period—a variance of just 0.6 percentage points.

General Commentary - One of the defining characteristics of TurboRegime is its willingness to remain patient. Rather than attempting to trade every market swing, the strategy waits for meaningful changes in market conditions before adjusting portfolio allocations. That disciplined approach has successfully navigated RiskOff, RiskOn, and RiskOn++ (Turbo) regimes during the first 104 days of live trading. While individual trades will naturally experience periods of both strength and weakness, the strategy remains focused on compounding returns over complete market cycles through consistent, objective decision-making. The continued alignment between live performance and historical backtesting provides additional confidence that the strategy is behaving as designed under real-world market conditions.
